channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I asked Gemini to play a text-based adventure game with me and the AI whisked me away to a word-based fantasy
item.description: Google Gemini is pretty good at created games on the fly – even ones that might remind you of the classics, like Zork.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: 16 hijacked browser extensions expose 3.2 million users
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy recommends deleting 16 browser extensions immediately in response to a report saying over 3.2 million web users were affected by a security breach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brother denies claims it locked down third-party printer ink cartridges via forced firmware updates
item.description: Some users are reporting print quality issues with third-party ink issues, but Brother says it has nothing to do with it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Spotify is leaning hard into video podcasts. Here’s how creators are adapting
item.description: Spotify just opened up a new stream of revenue for podcasters. That is, if they’re uploading video. What was once an audio-first medium, podcasting is now increasingly filmed and produced. That started on YouTube, which is now racking up one billion podcast viewers a month.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: This is Amazon's first foray in servers, and certainly not the last: MicroTik franken-router is powered by the AWS Graviton 1 Arm CPU
item.description: MicroTik's RDS2216 data server has 16 ports and is powered by Amazon's Graviton 1 Arm CPU used on AWS.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: The EU's Quest for Cloud Sovereignty: A New Era of Interoperability
item.description: The European Union's efforts to break free from US cloud domination have reached a significant milestone with the launch of the Sovereign European Cloud API (SECA). This new initiative aims to promote interoperability among rival cloud providers, making it easier for organizations to adopt hybrid or multicloud approaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NordVPN reacts to results from its latest security audit
item.description: The independent security check conucted by Cure53 comes only a few weeks after a successful no-log audit. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: AMD might go for Nvidia’s jugular in Q2 with a faster RX 9070 ‘Extreme’ GPU that could leave the RTX 5070 Ti in the dust
item.description: If AMD has Nvidia on the ropes now, rumored RX 9070 XTX model that’s faster than the RTX 5070 Ti could be a knockout blow.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: California biotech company gets FDA approval for EpiPen alternative for kids
item.description: The newly approved dosage of epinephrine nasal spray, Neffy, is geared toward children as young as 4.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha, The Mercury News

item.title: Davos 2025: Key priorities and truths for CIOs
item.description: Davos 2025 was a blend of optimism and urgency. One data point presented at the event showed that 62% of business leaders from large organizations expressed confidence in their growth prospects (Capgemini Research Institute).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: i2CAT promueve el primer laboratorio abierto europeo de investigación 6G en órbita baja
item.description: El centro de investigación i2CAT ha anunciado en el marco del Mobile World Congress la puesta en marcha del primer laboratorio en órbita terrestre baja de Europa (LEO, por sus siglas en inglés).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 8 obstacles women still face when seeking a leadership role in IT
item.description: If you are a tech leader, you might encourage your daughter to follow your path, imagining a journey, like yours, with challenges that can be overcome with hard work. But if you are a man — especially a white man — you are likely unaware of the massive obstacles she will face that you didn’t.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.98 shines on GitHub Copilot
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.98 has arrived, featuring enhancements to the GitHub Copilot AI coding assistant, including previews of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, Copilot agent mode improvements, and Copilot Edits support for notebook files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utah becomes first state to pass app store age verification law
item.description: Utah passed a bill on Wednesday that will require app stores to verify users' ages and receive parental consent for minors to download applications, making the Beehive State the first in the country to pass such a measure. The bill, titled the App Store Accountability Act, now heads to Utah Gov.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How Trump's tariffs could pinch the tech sector
item.description: The tech industry is set to feel the pinch as the Trump administration levies new tariffs against key U.S. trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump calls CHIPS Act ‘horrible,’ wants to defund it
item.description: In his address to Congress last night, President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act “horrible,” and said whatever money hasn’t been spent should now be used to reduce the national debt. The CHIPS Act, enacted into law in 2022 under then-President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why you should use dependency injection
item.description: A few weeks ago, I wrote about how a good software team will defer decisions as long as possible. Smart teams will design and build systems that don’t lock them into any particular implementation until it is utterly necessary.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New malware exploits fake updates to steal data
item.description: New malware "FrigidStealer" targets Mac computers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers four ways to stay safe from infostealer malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation
